results.possibleIdentity: Joan Webster Price (born 1931) is an American abstract artist, printmaker, and educator who fits the description of being active in the late 1960s and 1970s and associated with interdisciplinary art-technology circles. She earned an Ed.D. from Columbia University and served as a professor in the Art Department at the City College of New York (CCNY), where she was involved in curricula related to digital design and the intersection of art and science. A member of the American Abstract Artists (AAA), her work—which often explores geometric abstraction and spatial dynamics—is held in the permanent collections of major institutions including the Whitney Museum of American Art, the Museum of Modern Art (MoMA), and the Smithsonian American Art Museum. She is frequently cited in academic journals such as Leonardo and MIT Press publications alongside pioneers of art and technology (e.g., in discussions of 'metadesign'), which aligns with the mission of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.).
